mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate consistently predicts deteriorating corporate earnings and consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. The signal is always active regardless of regime.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC is not consistently negative over any 2-year period, or 20-day returns after UNRATE increases are not significantly below the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 1.0%, monetary policy is aggressively accommodative. The compression of discount rates and lower borrowing costs boost equity valuations and economic activity, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the hit ratio of positive returns is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month declines in both nonfarm payrolls and real retail sales signal simultaneous labor market weakness and collapsing consumer demand. This synchronous contraction strongly predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 500k month-over-month and RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month
failure_condition: When both triggers fire simultaneously, 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative at the 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: During a recession regime defined by unemployment above 6%, declining housing starts reveal deepening weakness in residential investment and consumer confidence. This additional demand-side deterioration pushes S&P 500 returns further negative over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In recession regimes, 20-day forward returns after HOUST declines (month-over-month) are not significantly more negative than in non-recession expansions (difference-in-means test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decrease of at least 25 basis points in the federal funds rate signals an intentional easing by the Fed. Markets react positively within one week as liquidity expectations improve and risk appetite increases, driving short-term S&P 500 gains.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS decreases by at least 25bp from previous month
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns after a 25bp+ cut are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the mean return is below the unconditional 5-day mean return.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in real retail sales reflects robust consumer spending, the primary driver of U.S. GDP. This positive demand momentum supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The Spearman correlation between RSAFS month-over-month change and forward 60-day SPX returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is below 4% and the federal funds rate is below 2%, the economy exhibits late-cycle conditions with tight labor markets and easy money. Excess leverage and speculative bubbles build, making equities vulnerable to a sharp reversal over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with low unemployment and easy monetary policy
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns when both conditions hold are not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the negative return frequency is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: During a regime of rising inflation (CPI YoY > 2%), a decline in industrial production month-over-month signals stagflation: weakening output combined with persistent price pressures. This combination erodes profit margins and forward earnings expectations, depressing S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In episodes where CPIAUCSL YoY > 2%, 20-day returns after INDPRO declines are not significantly more negative than in low-inflation regimes (difference-in-means p > 0.10).
